title: getLastElement returns undefined for non-empty arrays
body:
## Summary
`getLastElement` returns `undefined` for populated arrays instead of the final item.

## Reproduction
1. Open `src/utils.ts`.
2. Call `getLastElement([1, 2, 3])`.
3. Observe that the return value is `undefined` instead of `3`.

## Expected
Return the last array element rather than reading past the end of the array.

title: processItems crashes when items is null
body:
## Summary
`processItems` throws when the input is `null` or `undefined` instead of safely returning `0`.

## Reproduction
1. Open `src/utils.ts`.
2. Call `processItems(null)` or `processItems(undefined)`.
3. Observe that the function throws before returning a count.

## Expected
Handle missing input before reading `.length`.

title: checkEqual reports different numbers as equal
body:
## Summary
`checkEqual` treats different numbers as equal because it assigns inside the condition instead of comparing values.

## Reproduction
1. Open `src/utils.ts`.
2. Call `checkEqual(3, 7)`.
3. Observe that the function returns `true` instead of `false`.

## Expected
Use a comparison operator so unequal inputs return `false`.
